name: JBL Tour One M2 Wireless Over-Ear Headphones
whatWorks: 50h battery (ANC off) is among the best in the over-ear category — for frequent travelers and commuters who forget to charge, the Tour One M2 delivers a level of battery anxiety-free use that Sony and Bose can't match.

JBL Personi-Fi 2.0 runs a personalized hearing test and calibrates EQ to your specific hearing profile — the improvement in perceived clarity is noticeable for most users, particularly at the frequency range where individual hearing varies most.
Multipoint Bluetooth allows simultaneous connection to a laptop and phone — calls switch instantly between devices without manual re-pairing, which is genuinely useful for work-from-home setups.
Adaptive ANC with three modes (everyday, travel, and off) handles both office noise and airplane engine hum well. Performance sits between the Bose QC45 and Sony XM6 in real-world commuting tests.
worthKnowing: The Personi-Fi calibration test takes 5 minutes and is worthwhile but opt-in — buyers who skip it get a slightly V-shaped sound signature (boosted bass and highs) that not everyone prefers.
At 289g, these are heavier than the Sony WH-1000XM6 (254g) and Bose QC45 (238g). Extended sessions of 4+ hours cause noticeable fatigue for some listeners.
The JBL app is functional but has been inconsistently reviewed for stability — force quits and occasional connection drops to the app have been reported across iOS and Android.
ANC, while good, doesn't quite reach the Sony WH-1000XM6 level of environmental noise reduction in loud transit environments. The gap is small but real at the price point.

commonComplaints.title: Personi-Fi test takes too long and…
commonComplaints.body: Personi-Fi test takes too long and I had to redo it twice because the app crashed — eventually gave up and use the default.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy on the head after a…
commonComplaints.body: Heavy on the head after a few hours — I got headaches on long flights that I don't get with my lighter Bose.

commonComplaints.title: App crashes randomly on Android which…
commonComplaints.body: App crashes randomly on Android which is frustrating when you want to adjust ANC on the fly.

commonComplaints.title: ANC is good but not Sony…
commonComplaints.body: ANC is good but not Sony XM6 level — subway and loud environments still bleed through more than I wanted.

verdict: The JBL Tour One M2 is the strongest JBL has ever been in the premium over-ear category, with 50h battery, Personi-Fi sound personalization, and competitive adaptive ANC. Battery life is the clear standout — nothing in this price range lasts as long. The weight and app stability issues are real drawbacks for long listening sessions and power users. For buyers who prioritize battery endurance and personalized sound over the last word in ANC, this is the best JBL headphone available.

realBuyers.quote: I used these for an entire 14-hour flight and landed with battery to spare — 50 hours is not a marketing number, it's real.
The Personi-Fi test actually made a noticeable difference — everything sounds clearer and more defined at my specific settings.
Multipoint is flawless — calls come through on my laptop and it switches to my phone instantly when someone calls.
Best JBL headphones ever made. Not the best over-ears money can buy but the battery is unbeatable.

metaDescription: JBL Tour One M2 review for Canada: 50h battery, Personi-Fi personalized EQ, adaptive ANC, multipoint. Honest on weight and app stability. ~$349 CAD.
introText: The JBL Tour One M2 are premium over-ear headphones leading with extraordinary 50h battery life and Personi-Fi 2.0 personalized sound calibration — JBL's most capable headphones to date. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B0BXT95KDF) at around $349 CAD.
